pi_items.source: task.json description and setup_task.sh instructions explicitly require splitting the audio into 5 segments.
setup_task.sh field notes generation explicitly defines these three codes, and verifier.py checks for them.
setup_task.sh field notes generation explicitly defines BKGD for background/ambient.
Calculated from split times (6s, 12s, 18s, 24s) multiplied by the 44100Hz sample rate enforced by ffmpeg in setup_task.sh. Match exactly with verifier.py expected_ranges.
setup_task.sh enforces a sample rate of 44100Hz via the ffmpeg -ar 44100 argument.
verifier.py checks if score >= 55.0 for the task to pass.

privileged_info_summary: The field recording data is synthetically generated by copying and resampling an existing generic audio file (e.g. moonlight_sonata.wav) to exactly 30 seconds at 44100Hz. The 5 expected regions, split position samples [264600, 529200, 793800, 1058400] based on 6-second intervals at 44.1kHz, species codes ['AMRO', 'MOCH', 'YWAR'], background code 'BKGD', and passing score threshold of 55 are all verified by examining the task setup scripts and verifier logic.
